mode_selection_rationale: Operator-directive discovery pre-flight found 1 pending directive (DIRECTIVE-20260513-002-split-analyst-baby-vs-deep, priority=medium, filed 2026-05-13T17:35Z). Per tinker.md dispatcher: 'directives override Mode 1-4 selection logic entirely'. Routed primary work to PROP authoring per directive task list. Mode 1/2 quick checks ran in parallel and pipeline is healthy aside from the backlog-trend operator_escalation tier firing on assigned_analyst_count > 150 (the empirical bottleneck the directive addresses).

modes_checked.mode_1_pipeline_health: GREEN — no stranded patches >24h old (none present), all agents producing fresh outputs, latest analyst run 2026-05-13T19:58Z (4h cadence on time), latest decider 2026-05-13T21:13Z (4h cadence on time), workspace-sync hourly at :03 running.
modes_checked.mode_2_infra_fuse: GREEN — project footprint 564MB (warning tier ≥500MB; CRITICAL ≥1000MB) but composition is mostly FUSE workspace (~560MB) which is the workspace itself, not an accumulated clone leak. No /tmp/*-clone or /tmp/*-clone-* directories present. /tmp/work and /tmp/kev-repo are non-project content (~149MB combined) unrelated to dome pipeline.
modes_checked.mode_3_cost_engineering: DEFERRED to next non-directive-run; directive primary today.

details.trend_36h: assigned_analyst stuck at ~151 despite +135 net closure 7d; age_ge_14d rising 31→78 over 36h. The system is net-closing but the analyst queue is structurally stuck.

findings.action_taken: Operator-directive DIRECTIVE-20260513-002 (filed 2026-05-13T17:35Z) targeting exactly this bottleneck was picked up this run. PROP-034 authored (split-analyst design with phased deployment recommendation and counter-argument analysis).

findings.directive_title: Split analyst into baby-analyst (Sonnet, hourly, BAU drain) + analyst (Opus, 4h, deep work)
findings.headline_recommendation: APPROVE phased deployment (Phase 1 Mode 1 BAU drain only, Sonnet 2h cadence) — NOT full directive-as-stated (hourly + Mode 2b + Mode 1b verification). Phase 1 success gates Phase 2; Phase 3 (Mode 1b prediction class) is speculative pending a new prediction_class schema.
findings.key_counter_argument: Recommend operator try a cheaper alternative FIRST: bump existing analyst to 2h Opus cadence as a 3-day experiment. If that alone drains the orphan backlog meaningfully, defer the split. If 3 days of 2h Opus doesn't drain it (most likely outcome — Opus's dispatcher gravity toward 'interesting work' is the issue, not just the cadence), proceed to Phase 1 split.

findings.directive_premise: analyst's Mode 1 BAU drain rarely wins the dispatcher slot

tinker_measured.queue_state_evidence: assigned_analyst_count flat at ~151 over 36+ hours despite healthy 135/7d net closure

findings.interpretation: The empirical premise of the directive is confirmed. Today's pattern (BAU drain wins 1 of 5 dispatches) plus the flat queue trend supports the structural-bottleneck framing.
